hero.title.name: Aleksander Zolotarev.
hero.subtitle: I’m a front-end web developer
project.infologistic24.title: Infologistic 24
project.infologistic24.description:
Worked on new modules and features for the administrative panel of a logistics service using React and TypeScript.
Built interactive components for tables, filters, search, sorting, pagination and operator workflows.
Analyzed existing projects and improved interface solutions based on business requirements and team feedback.
Contributed to codebase optimization, improving component readability and reusability.
Fixed defects in data rendering, validation and REST API interactions.
Maintained code quality by following consistency principles, supporting code style and improving the frontend structure.
Wrote tests for key interface modules using Jest and React Testing Library.
Participated in code reviews, technical discussions, Scrum sprints, task planning and release preparation.
Worked on safer client-side development: input validation, correct error handling and interface resilience for incorrect scenarios.
Helped migrate individual modules and update components for new product requirements.
Optimized tables and large data lists, reducing render time and interface load.
Developed reusable filtering and search logic with debounce, reducing unnecessary server requests.
Contributed to analytical interfaces in the admin panel: tables, filters, search screens and overview pages.
project.kaskad-davtian.title: Kaskad & Davtian
project.kaskad-davtian.description:
Corporate website for a construction company with two directions:
Kaskad — construction, landscaping and infrastructure projects
Davtian Architects — an architectural studio and in-house design department.
The main concept is a full-cycle, turnkey approach: from design and planning to the final construction stage.
I implemented the UI with the brand colors (brown for Kaskad and emerald for Davtian), integrated Strapi CMS, built the API and database layer, and set up a convenient system for adding and managing projects.
The solution is fast, stable, and easy to update via the admin panel.
project.blossom.title: Blossom
project.blossom.description:
E-commerce website for selling curtains, built on the InSales platform.
I developed and configured the project fully turnkey: from the product catalog structure to the final checkout flow.
A custom price calculator was implemented, allowing customers to calculate the product cost based on dimensions and parameters.
CDEK delivery service was fully integrated, along with payment methods and order processing logic.
The product database, categories and attributes were fully structured and configured.
The solution is ready for scaling and easy to manage through the administrative panel.
project.aiscreen.title: AIScreen
project.aiscreen.description:
Corporate website for an advanced digital signage platform.
I fully implemented the website turnkey using WordPress: from structure and visual presentation to content management and performance optimization.
The site showcases a powerful digital signage solution that turns any TV into a managed screen without additional hardware.
I structured and presented complex product features including template libraries, app integrations, split-screen zones, device management, and real-time content updates.
The project highlights cross-platform support, cloud-based screen control, and enterprise-ready functionality.
The website is optimized for scalability, easy content updates, and clear communication of the product’s value to businesses worldwide.
project.renda.title: Renda
project.renda.description:
A service for automating business processes of management companies, tenants and owners of premises. 
What i did: Implemented visualization of floor plans with polygons, worked with the Swagger REST API, developed individual management pages, made changes to existing modules.
project.cleverrenta.title: Cleverrenta
project.cleverrenta.description:
I have developed a full-fledged web-based platform for renting and selling furniture with a client side, an admin panel and an internal logistics module. The project combines an online store, CRM/ERP logic and warehouse accounting in one interface: the user can select goods, place an order, specify the rental period, delivery or pickup, and employees manage goods, balances, orders and statuses.
The system is logically similar to internal 1C/ERP solutions: there are user roles, closed administrative sections, warehouse accounting, catalog management, order processing, logistics, integration with external services and automatic sending of email notifications. Email code authorization, access control for the administrator and the logistician, server-side work with sessions and secure APIs have been implemented.
In the project, I made not just a showcase website, but a working business tool: a product catalog, filtering, shopping cart, order processing, administrative management of goods, categories, tags, attributes, images, warehouses and leftovers. This format is suitable for a real rental business, where it is important to simultaneously conduct sales, rent, warehouse, delivery and internal processes of the company.

about.description: Hi, my name is Alexander, and I'm a frontend developer passionate about design, interfaces and modern web technologies. I build responsive websites, complex UI animations, clean structured code and full-featured modern projects. I'm deeply interested in new technologies, clean architecture, and always learning tools that help improve both development quality and user experience.
